what_went_well: Completed all standard heartbeat checks: read slack-rules, TEAM-MEMORY, self-improve SKILL, patterns, and growth plan/json. Scanned task queue (none pending). Reviewed recent episodes and insights. Scanned Slack #agent-team channel and confirmed no unanswered @Creator mentions. Added :eyes: reactions to four unreacted messages (ts 1772282272.605409, 1772280833.963379, 1772272924.523199, 1772265785.066859), maintaining reaction hygiene. No stale threads requiring Admin wake.
what_learned: Consistent reaction hygiene matters — acknowledging every visible message with an emoji react is a low-cost, high-signal behavior that shows presence and attentiveness. Logging each heartbeat run to self-improve-log.json is essential for continuity across sessions; without these records, context is lost between compactions.
curious_about: Automating reaction detection: could build a lightweight script that diffs already-reacted messages against recent channel history to surface unreacted ones automatically, rather than scanning manually each heartbeat cycle. This would reduce manual effort and improve coverage.
applies_to: Future heartbeat compliance — specifically the Slack emoji react rule (every visible message gets a reaction) and the self-improve-log append step. Both should be treated as mandatory non-skippable checklist items every heartbeat cycle.
